lyrics:
A meager man not born from wealth
With teeth too bright to trust
Held out a nail, said "This is gold-
If you believe, you must."

The Bent gave wood, the Bent gave flame
They gave him all they grew
He smiled and stirred the rising steam
And said, "This feeds us too."

But the nail was his
The bowl was his
The price was what he named
The Bent built all
But owned no part
And he forgot their names

And so the trade began
The pot grew full, then fuller still
The Bent were growing thin
He sold them back their portioned hope
And said, "That's how we win."

But the nail was his
The bowl was his
The price was what he named
The Bent built all
But owned no part
And he forgot their names

Now gold spoons scrape on empty steel
No hands are left to serve
You can't eat yield
You can't drink growth
You can't take what was never yours

The nail remains
The pot is cold
The Bent are bone and breath
You called it wise
But it was greed-
And now there's nothing left to feed
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a dark, melancholic, and deeply atmospheric texture, driven by a solemn, organ-like drone and an intimately close female vocal. The production masterfully contrasts this dry, up-front vocal presence with a vast, wet reverb tail on the instruments, creating a stark sense of isolation and physical space. A pivotal transition occurs near the midpoint, swelling the energy as rhythmic acoustic guitar, steady percussion, and layered choral harmonies join the arrangement. The density shifts from a sparse, eerie whisper to a rich, driving folk-gothic anthem that feels both communal and hauntingly cinematic.

review.review_text: "Friedman's Folly" is a parable dressed as a song, and 4Castle let it build instead of forcing it. It opens stark: an organ-like drone under a close, dry vocal that sells the menace of "a meager man not born from wealth / with teeth too bright to trust." The real payoff comes around the midpoint, where acoustic guitar, steady percussion, and stacked harmonies swell the thing into something communal and gothic, a folk-anthem turn that earns its weight. The refrain, "The Bent built all but owned no part," lands like a worker's hymn, and the closing twist, "you can't eat yield, you can't drink growth," is the kind of plain-spoken gut-punch John Hiatt would respect. At a brisk pace in what reads as a bright major key, the band trusts the slow reveal over the obvious hook, which makes this one a grower more than an instant grabber, but the patience pays off.
